response:
the area of the regular hexagon with side length 30 meters is calculated by dividing the shape into six equilateral triangles where the area of one triangle is (sqrt(3)/4) * 30^2 which equals 225*sqrt(3) and multiplying by six gives a total hexagonal area of 1350*sqrt(3) square meters.

the radius of the circular garden touching the midpoints of the hexagon's sides corresponds to the apothem of the hexagon which is found using the formula (side * sqrt(3))/2 resulting in a radius of 15*sqrt(3) meters and the area of this circle is pi * (15*sqrt(3))^2 which simplifies to 675*pi square meters.
